stem: A agriculture study measures crop yield across 5 regions, each containing multiple farms. region A has 10 observations with mean 40.5 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 9.5). region B has 5 observations with mean 42.6 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 8.7). region C has 8 observations with mean 29.4 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 7.4). region D has 10 observations with mean 54.1 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 3.8). region E has 5 observations with mean 26.3 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 8.8). The between-region standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 6.5 and 16.2 tonnes/hectare. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true crop yield for region D, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-region variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for region D: 53.9 tonnes/hectare (shrunk from raw 54.1). Range [53.6, 54.0] as between-region SD varies from 6.5 to 16.2. Pooled mean: 40.1.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 40.1 tonnes/hectare (weighted average of 5 region means).
Shrinkage factor for region D: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/10). At σ_between=10.8: B = 0.988.
Interval [53.6, 54.0] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (54.1) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (40.1) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
